steps.title: Solve for x in terms of y using the simpler equation
steps.content:
The second equation is already linear and simple:
$$x + 2y = -12$$
Solve for $x$: $$x = -12 - 2y$$

steps.title: Substitute into the first equation to find y
steps.content:
Replace $x$ with $-12 - 2y$ in the first equation:
$$\frac{5}{2}(-12 - 2y) - \frac{1}{5}y = 48$$
Expand: $$-30 - 5y - \frac{1}{5}y = 48$$
Rearrange: $$-5y - \frac{1}{5}y = 78$$
Combine: $$-\frac{26}{5}y = 78$$
Solve: $$y = -15$$

steps.title: Back-substitute to find x and compute x + y
steps.content:
Substitute $y = -15$ into $x = -12 - 2y$:
$$x = -12 - 2(-15) = -12 + 30 = 18$$
Therefore: $$x + y = 18 + (-15) = 3$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
